jd.title: Senior Program Manager — Defense & Aerospace
jd.text:
Senior Program Manager — Defense & Aerospace
EDGE Group
Abu Dhabi, UAE

About EDGE:
EDGE is the UAE's leading advanced technology group for defense and beyond. We develop innovative solutions across five core clusters: Platforms & Systems, Missiles & Weapons, Electronic Warfare & Cyber Technologies, Mission Support, and Trading & Mission Support.

Role Summary:
We are seeking a Senior Program Manager to lead complex defense acquisition and development programs from inception through delivery. You will manage cross-functional teams, international partners, and government stakeholders to deliver mission-critical systems on time and within budget.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead end-to-end program execution for defense platform development and integration programs ($10M-$100M value)
- Manage program scope, schedule, cost, and risk using EVM methodology
- Chair program reviews (PDR, CDR, TRR, FQR) and present to executive leadership
- Coordinate cross-functional IPTs (Integrated Product Teams) across engineering, test, manufacturing, and logistics
- Manage international partnerships and OEM relationships (technology transfer, co-development)
- Drive configuration management and change control processes
- Oversee systems integration, V&V, and acceptance testing activities
- Prepare and manage RAID logs, risk registries, and mitigation plans
- Support capture and proposal activities for new business opportunities
- Ensure compliance with defense standards (MIL-STD, DO-178C, DO-254)

Required Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Aerospace, Electrical, Mechanical, or Systems)
- 12+ years of experience in defense/aerospace program management
- PMP or equivalent certification
- Experience managing programs with international partners
- Strong knowledge of EVM, configuration management, and defense acquisition lifecycle
- Experience with systems integration and test campaigns
- Excellent stakeholder management and executive communication skills

Preferred:
- Master's degree in Engineering or MBA
- PMI-ACP or Agile certification
- Experience with fighter aircraft or UAV programs
- Arabic language skills
- Active security clearance (UAE or transferable)

Benefits:
- Competitive tax-free salary
- Housing and transport allowance
- Annual flights + 30 days leave
- World-class facilities and R&D environment

key_matches: EVM methodology (directly mentioned across PMO roles)
RAID logs and risk management (directly mentioned)
International partnerships: China/CADI JF-17 joint development
Configuration management and change control (core competency)
MIL-STD, DO-178C, DO-254 compliance (listed in competencies)
Fighter aircraft programs: JF-17 Thunder (directly relevant)
IPT leadership across engineering, test, manufacturing
gaps_to_bridge: No explicit $10M-$100M budget numbers — bridge: 'JF-17 program is multi-billion, managed work packages within it'
No PDR/CDR/TRR/FQR review chair experience listed explicitly — bridge: 'led program reviews (equivalent gates in military lifecycle)'
No capture/proposal experience listed — bridge: 'supported export programs to 11 countries, government-to-government negotiations'
UAE security clearance needed — bridge: 'held Pakistan military clearance, transferable process familiar'
expected_output_notes.position_assessment: Strong position — direct match on nearly all requirements, defense program management is core career
cv_sections_to_emphasize: Deputy Director PMO JF-17 (Role #7) — most senior PM role
Asst Director PMO JF-17 (Role #5) — continuity shows progression
Glass cockpit export to 11 countries — captures/BD adjacent
Both PMP and PMI-ACP in certifications
